what_it_is: A fail-closed wording-authority gate. It builds a canonical plain-English dictionary index from an A-Z doctrine batch, then regex-lints any file for retired aliases, banned register, overclaims, private-only leakage, and undefined Title-Case/ALLCAPS system terms; a deterministic agent pass rewrites to public phrasing and emits a review sidecar. Every scan writes a JSON receipt and returns exit 1 on FAIL (and on WARN when strict), which is what git/Cursor hooks enforce.

current_state.note: Core engine is operational: `python3 language_gate/language_gate_pipeline_v1.py <file>` runs, dictionary_index.json holds 225 terms + 10 aliases, tool_version is language_gate_rc3_pile_v1, and a full 160-file library scan receipt exists (3 FAIL / 87 WARN / 34 PASS_WITH_REWRITE / 36 PASS). Enforcement is wired outside scope via .cursor/hooks.json (afterFileEdit + preToolUse block) and .githooks/pre-commit installed by scripts/install_language_gate_hooks_v1.sh (confirmed present, not deep-read per scope). Two integration gaps: (1) the two overlay index JSONs are produced but ORPHANED — no core/pipeline/agent code loads them; (2) no automated test harness exists despite test_files/ fixtures. Only dictionary_rc2_supplement.json is actually merged at load time.

key_assets.purpose: THE enforcement engine: SURFACES, SURFACE_POLICY toggles, load_dictionary (merges rc2 supplement), scan() (alias/synonym/banned/overclaim/private/undefined findings), decide() (FAIL/WARN/PASS_WITH_REWRITE/PASS), write_receipt(). Holds the big hardcoded allowlists (STATUS_LABEL, CENSUS_VERB, FRAGMENT, TECH_REFERENCE, STRUCTURAL, ENTITY, vendor).

Orchestrator: infer_surface -> scan -> regex rewrite -> agent plain_english_pass -> write_receipt; exit_code() returns 1 on FAIL and on WARN when strict_undefined. Flags: --surface --write --skip-agent --soft-undefined --json.
Deterministic plain-English pass using dictionary public_phrasing + jargon table; write_sidecar() produces the <file>.language_gate_review.json sidecars (agent_review + dictionary_warnings).
Parses NOETFIELD_DICTIONARY_BATCH_A-Z_v1.md into dictionary_index.json: terms, alias_map, retired_terms, private_only, code_alias (+ legacy tombstone_map).
Reverse direction — regenerates the A-Z batch markdown from the index with CANONICAL_PROMOTIONS and SYNONYM_ALIASES, so plain-English canonical wins and old jargon becomes retired aliases.
Live compiled dictionary: 225 terms, 10-entry alias_map, 13 retired, 3 private_only, 2 code_alias. Loaded by every scan.
The ONE live extension file: 43 gap-fill terms + 48-item structural_allowlist + alias_index, merged in load_dictionary() and effective_structural_allowlist().
127 SourceA overlay entries classed COMMAND_FRAGMENT/FORM_OPTION/STATUS_LABEL/SOURCEA_LOCAL_TERM with cleanup_rules — produced but NOT consumed by the gate (orphaned).
38 TrustField overlay entries (DRAFT) including CONFLICT_PHRASE and REGULATORY_COPY_RISK classes (e.g. RPAA MSP/PSP self-claim guards) — produced but NOT consumed by the gate (orphaned).
Read-only harness scanning 5 controlled canon files across surfaces; writes receipts/rc2_dry_scan_report.json (currently all PASS).
Proves the gate reads the dictionary from disk (SHA of index+batch) with no chat memory; emits a cold-session proof receipt.

Aggregate 160-file library scan output (file/surface/decision/findings_count/regex_would_change/agent_would_change) — shows the schema a library-scan runner emits, but that runner is NOT in language_gate/.
Fixtures: clean_example.md, violations_example.md, tombstone_only.md, cold_session_doctrine_sample_v1.md — used manually; no assertion harness runs them.
Authority chain, surface table, pipeline usage, hook wiring, RC2 fixes, strict-vs-soft undefined policy.

extension_points: effective_structural_allowlist() / effective_entity_allowlist() in language_gate_core_v1.py — the merge point that today only pulls supplement.structural_allowlist; the natural hook to fold in overlay-index entries
load_dictionary() SUPPLEMENT_PATH merge — add terms/alias_map/code_alias without editing code (rc2 supplement is the working template)
SURFACE_POLICY dict (internal/public/website/contract/prompt/receipt) — per-surface block_* toggles are data-shaped and easy to extend with a new surface or policy flag
Regex tables SYNONYM_AUTOFIX, BANNED_REGISTER, OVERCLAIM_PATTERNS — pattern lists that new wording rules plug into
write_receipt() -> receipts/*.receipt.json and write_sidecar() -> *.language_gate_review.json — structured outputs any aggregator/dashboard can consume
infer_surface() path-prefix rules — where new path->surface mappings register
The two overlay index JSONs (sourcea/trustfield) — already built to a stable schema (entries[].term/class/note) but not yet wired into scan()

buildable_ideas.idea: Wire the orphaned overlay indexes into the gate: extend effective_structural_allowlist()/effective_entity_allowlist() to fold overlay entries[] by class (COMMAND_FRAGMENT, FORM_OPTION, STATUS_LABEL, *_LOCAL_TERM -> skip as undefined; keep CONFLICT_PHRASE/REGULATORY_COPY_RISK separate). Kills UNDEFINED_TERM noise the overlays were built to suppress and finally connects 165 produced-but-unused entries.

Add a pytest regression harness (language_gate/test_language_gate_v1.py) that runs the pipeline over test_files/ and asserts expected decisions: violations_example -> FAIL with the exact BANNED/OVERCLAIM findings, clean_example -> PASS, tombstone_only -> autofix. Locks current behavior before anyone touches the allowlists; today nothing guards the gate.

Build an in-gate read-only library scan runner (language_gate/library_scan_v1.py) that globs a path, calls scan() with write=False, and emits the existing library_scan details.json schema plus a decision rollup. Re-owns the capability that produced the 160-file receipt (whose runner is not in the gate) so the 3 FAIL / 87 WARN can be re-derived and tracked.

Promote TrustField overlay REGULATORY_COPY_RISK and CONFLICT_PHRASE entries into surface-scoped overclaim/banned patterns (e.g. block MSP/PSP self-claims on website/public), driven by the overlay JSON instead of the hardcoded OVERCLAIM_PATTERNS list. Turns the DRAFT TrustField overlay into real enforcement for regulated web copy.

Externalize the six large hardcoded allowlists (STATUS_LABEL/CENSUS_VERB/FRAGMENT/TECH_REFERENCE/STRUCTURAL/ENTITY) out of language_gate_core_v1.py into a JSON allowlist file loaded like dictionary_rc2_supplement.json, so vocabulary edits stop requiring Python changes and stay reviewable as data.

Build a simple static HTML rollup (per founder 'simple web format' rule) that reads receipts/*.receipt.json and *.language_gate_review.json sidecars into one board: decision counts, top undefined terms, open agent_review flags, worst offending files. Gives visibility over gate output with no new enforcement surface.
